name: MJ Hegar Battleground HQ
subject: Comparing records
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
We need someone in the Senate who knows what life is like for working Texans -- not someone who's spent 18 years selling out Texans to the special interests.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890212/7f51f7bc/4c1c864e/4ef1e4fd/1669885812/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Peter, we think that when you compare MJ's record with Sen. Cornyn's, it's pretty obvious that we need MJ to replace him. If you agree: **[donate now to help make it happen.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890212/7f51f7bc/4c1c864e/4ef1e4fc/1669885812/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTQifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200322.3nd__1094__&amount=10&recurring=true")** | **Here 's what MJ has been up to for the past 20 years:**
* Flew search and rescue missions in Afghanistan, earning a Purple Heart and Distinguished Flying Cross with Valor for her heroism after the Taliban shot down her helicopter
* Took on the Pentagon to open ground combat roles to women, advocating to _both_ sides of the aisle
* Raising two young boys as a working mom after life-threatening pregnancies -- **so you can count on MJ to protect and improve our health care**

---

**And here 's what Sen. Cornyn has been up to during the same time:**
* Raising tons of money from private detention companies, big pharma, and the gun lobby
* Trying to repeal health care protections for Texans -- twenty times
* Giving the White House the green light to confiscate private land from Texans to build Trump's border wall

---
We need someone in the Senate who knows what life is like for working Texans -- not someone who's spent 18 years selling out Texans to the special interests. We need MJ.

**[Will you help Texas get the Senator we deserve and donate today?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890212/7f51f7bc/4c1c864e/4ef1e4fc/1669885812/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTQifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200322.3nd__1094__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

\-- Team MJ

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890212/7f51f7bc/4c1c864e/4ef1e4fc/1669885812/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTQifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200322.3nd__1094__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

|

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: FWD: Unprecedented
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
_We know this is a difficult time, so please take care of yourself and your loved ones and only pitch in if you can afford to do so. To pause our emails until this public health emergency has passed,[fill out this form](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771318/691c9bd/68a2fc72/11a7605d/275596565/VEsH/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://jhfp.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/covid-19?source=em200319")._ Hey there, I wanted to make sure you saw my note from yesterday. This pandemic is an unprecedented moment in our nation's history and I know the toll it's taking on all of us. Now more than ever, we need to come together and get things done for the American people. **Read my note below and then,_only_ if you are financially able, please pitch in a donation to help our campaign bring about the change Coloradans deserve.** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.%3D/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200319-bump?refcode=em-200319-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

---
Thank you,
John

* * *
Folks -- I want to be honest with you for a moment, if you can spare a few minutes to let me explain something. Here it goes:
These are not normal times. It would be wrong to pretend they are. Our nation is facing an unprecedented public health emergency that is impacting virtually every facet of American society right now.
Our campaign has canceled all in-person events, including our fundraisers, and it means we're engaging in a different kind of campaign. But although the tactics are different, our campaign's message is not:
**This crisis is a reminder that we need to come together more than ever to get things done. That 's how we improved our state's economy here in Colorado coming out of the Great Recession, and it's how our country will survive the COVID-19 outbreak, too.**
I am writing to you today to ask you to contribute to our campaign if you believe our country is ready to put aside our partisan blinders and get to work on the critical issues that matter, from addressing this pandemic to climate change and building an economy that works for everyone.
I am asking you to chip in today, but I only want you to do it if you are financially able to. I really mean that, folks. So, I'm asking:
**If you are financially able, please make a contribution of $10 or more to our campaign today to help us flip this Senate seat and get our government working for the people again:**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://secure.hickenlooper.%3D/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jwh-em-200319-bump?refcode=em-200319-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

I know how isolated these times may feel for so many of you, but know you are not alone in this fight. We are going to get through this together.
Thanks and stay safe.
-- John

Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado

name: Terry Neese
subject: BREAKING NEWS: Neese Poll Shows Trump Up Big
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Neese for Congress [Unsubscribe](http://sendy
body:
|
---
Friend, We just received an exciting piece of news that I can't wait to share with you! **My team recently conducted a poll of voters in our district, and the results we got back are incredibly exciting. ** With an approval rating of 86% for President Trump and a 73% approval rating for Governor Stitt, **it 's clear the people of Oklahoma believe in a conservative agenda! ** Kendra Horn has stood against President Trump and his America First agenda in Congress at every turn. **She has proven time after time we cannot count on her to represent us or our shared values.** But if we're going to beat Kendra Horn and take back the House in November, we need someone we can trust to support President Trump. **I 'm the only candidate in this race with a proven record of supporting President Trump.****** In 2016, I served as a co-chair of his Main Street Business Council and as a member of the Trump transition team. In Congress, I will stand with him to advance his agenda to Keep America Great. [**Can we count on you to join our team and help President Trump send a conservative ally to Congress?**](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/kQuDPoZT0892iF1HpSDpzb1g/2CqEj6ZdVaoC26B5763oCnBQ/AB6D4kq6gIe2mm0agOmIOA) [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/kQuDPoZT0892iF1HpSDpzb1g/2CqEj6ZdVaoC26B5763oCnBQ/AB6D4kq6gIe2mm0agOmIOA) [**If we 're going to replace Kendra Horn with a conservative champion, we need your help TODAY. Will you join our campaign by chipping in $20.20 right now?**](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/kQuDPoZT0892iF1HpSDpzb1g/2CqEj6ZdVaoC26B5763oCnBQ/AB6D4kq6gIe2mm0agOmIOA) I hope we can count on your support today, friend. Terry Neese

name: Doyle Canning for Congress
subject: [The Oregonizer] Climate Leaders Back Canning...Pass it On.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CANNING FOR CONGRESS ](https://u1584542
body:
Doyle Canning came straight out of the climate movement--and that's a perspective we sorely need in DC. Elections are about the future, and the future is about making big strides to cool the planet. | |
---
|
---
| [ ](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HgE/ni0YAA/t.308/AiMuo7JyS82qSjtphq1jJg/h0/Y-2FgbIxpjl7vCXwYlxcWe74cJIRNk1BsdXc03JixbMX3KgWMCbbSCydC3bZxBc1tJx4r5FFv8Wllz735lRuDeCmNdVh30DeJPGn5SbQijEHgcLiZgnnaLZuYvabrQLs7oMjrblAepN1BW3nPV3FLB3RBVO2nQMFJvGFT72x1nYRsDwQ6lMmhfwxooTq2D0oUYOx1qhhTUU4yuvSs49li-2Fw8PsYGBvZ-2B5u1DpMAP3u7sDbbryeQF6MX9lxavBVuC5D0Y00yx9xH8fMxicOQi-2Bnhtqd9qNCXOFN-2BVxRLpDOzjmJ37vOMS5QVC9Mz0qgc189oogE73dLTS1zdtj9KOdSdku4Nu9G5b9ScfVrjHUGQV54iTDfPSrovgK2HqG8Oio8/7QS5)
---
"It is outrageous that in this time of crisis, when many people cannot even leave their homes safely, the Federal Government is prioritizing a fracked gas pipeline and export terminal that local communities and the State of Oregon have firmly said no to," said Sandy Lyon an impacted landowner in Douglas County. "Pembina can now attempt to use eminent domain against Oregon landowners, but construction cannot begin without state approval. We will stand firm against this project and we are counting on Oregon to do the same." I stand with Sandy, today - yesterday - tomorrow and always.
---
|
---
Dear supporters, This morning, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission decided to approve the permit for the Jordan Cove LNG project and Pacific Connector pipeline. Sandy hit the nail on the head: It is _outrageous_ , especially now in this time of national crisis. The fossil fuel industry will keep pushing pipelines forward until we have political leaders who push back. That's why I'm in this race and why I need your support now more than ever. Our mission is to serve the people of the fourth district of Oregon, and while Pete Defazio is taking meetings with the airline industry CEOs, his largest campaign donors, "at their request" to discuss a multi-billion dollar bailout in the name of the COVID crisis, we are talking with you about what's happening here, in Oregon, to regular people. We are hearing that you lost your job, that your hours were cut, that your graduation is cancelled, that you can't pay your rent, that your kids are home indefinitely, that you can't get a COVID test, that you're having a hard time. I understand. None of this is easy, and this is a moment that will reshape everything. Leadership matters, and I am committed to do every thing in my power to support our community through this extremely challenging time. To that end, my campaign is moving swiftly to launch a new virtual outreach program to respond to the crisis, and connect our community. Stay tuned, and stay with us. And please follow our Facebook Page for nightly "Corona Daily" roundups of news, resources, and inspiration you can use. Onward, Doyle
---

We need action at the national level, that is why I need all voters to support Doyle Canning for Congress. Right now, an individual working for minimum wage would have to work 67 hours per week to afford a modest one-bedroom rental home. Even two-income families would find it difficult to afford stable housing, paying close to or more than 30% of their income on housing. Housing is even more out of reach for historically marginalized communities like people of color, immigrants, individuals with disabilities and our LGBTQIA community. We need driven and active leadership instead of another two years of Rep. Peter DeFazio. Canning's housing plan includes building affordable housing units as part of the Green New Deal, investing in communities that eliminate local zoning density restrictions to bring in new affordable units and removing systemic barriers to homeownership in formerly redlined communities to close the homeownership gap between black and white families. With homelessness growing in our cities and rural areas, **it 's time to elect Canning.**" -Dana Kiernan, Eugene

Canning says she was 20 years old when her mother lost her job and died from a chronic condition because she didn't have access to health care. "These issues are personal, and Bernie has been the one standing for Medicare for All since I was a child," she says. "That consistency is what we need to defeat Donald Trump."

clean_body: Doyle Canning came straight out of the climate movement--and that's a perspective we sorely need in DC. Elections are about the future, and the future is about making big strides to cool the planet. | | | | "It is outrageous that in this time of crisis, when many people cannot even leave their homes safely, the Federal Government is prioritizing a fracked gas pipeline and export terminal that local communities and the State of Oregon have firmly said no to," said Sandy Lyon an impacted landowner in Douglas County. "Pembina can now attempt to use eminent domain against Oregon landowners, but construction cannot begin without state approval. We will stand firm against this project and we are counting on Oregon to do the same." I stand with Sandy, today - yesterday - tomorrow and always. | Dear supporters, This morning, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission decided to approve the permit for the Jordan Cove LNG project and Pacific Connector pipeline. Sandy hit the nail on the head: It is outrageous , especially now in this time of national crisis. The fossil fuel industry will keep pushing pipelines forward until we have political leaders who push back. That's why I'm in this race and why I need your support now more than ever. Our mission is to serve the people of the fourth district of Oregon, and while Pete Defazio is taking meetings with the airline industry CEOs, his largest campaign donors, "at their request" to discuss a multi-billion dollar bailout in the name of the COVID crisis, we are talking with you about what's happening here, in Oregon, to regular people. We are hearing that you lost your job, that your hours were cut, that your graduation is cancelled, that you can't pay your rent, that your kids are home indefinitely, that you can't get a COVID test, that you're having a hard time. I understand. None of this is easy, and this is a moment that will reshape everything. Leadership matters, and I am committed to do every thing in my power to support our community through this extremely challenging time. To that end, my campaign is moving swiftly to launch a new virtual outreach program to respond to the crisis, and connect our community. Stay tuned, and stay with us. And please follow our Facebook Page for nightly "Corona Daily" roundups of news, resources, and inspiration you can use. name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: BREAKING: Loeffler and Perdue dump stock in inside trading scheme while leaving America unprepared for coronavirus
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:

name: Adem Bunkeddeko
subject: We have less than 100 days to make it work
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/rZddt1k0uyJ5HKz27Snwii52WbyTRMczFPvcksd2nCE=)

Peter,

In less than 100 days, voters will be casting their ballots to elect their next Congress Member. But with New York City -- and cities around the world -- shutting down to prevent the spread of COVID-19, our campaign needs to adjust.

Last election, we came within approximately a thousand votes of winning because we outworked our opponent. But now, we have to outwork _and_ we have to get creative in how we campaign in order to prevent the spread of the virus and keep our community safe.

Instead of knocking every door, we need to text every phone. Instead of flyering every subway stop, we need to invest in digital ads. Instead of house parties, we need to have online events. And to do all of that, we need to raise enough money to invest in more expensive means of campaigning.

**[Will you rush a $100 donation right now so we have what it takes to run the strongest campaign in Brooklyn?](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=?amount=100)**

_If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
******[Express Donate: $200](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=?express_lane=true&amount=200&refcode=express_lane_200)******
******[Express Donate: $100](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100)******
******[Express Donate: $50](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50)******
******[Or, donate another amount](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other)******

Whether or not we're able to make these final months count will depend on the actions we take _right now_. We need to reach voters safely, ensure that people know there's an upcoming election, and continue making the case for why our movement is the one that's going to lead Brooklyn forward. There are many in our Brooklyn community who are struggling right now and need an active voice to advocate for them every single day. **[But I can't do it alone -- if we're going to bring real change to our communities, I'll need your help if you can offer it at this defining time.](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=)**

We're counting down the days until June 23rd, and there's no time to waste between now and then. **[If possible, will you donate $200, $100, or $50 today to ensure that these next 100 days illustrate the endurance and determination of our movement?](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=)**

Best,
Adem
******[DONATE TODAY](https://act.myngp.com/el/ViMP54aIrI0Q17-Q4LWiiI3_QdC43FaOPSn4hN-5MzQ=/wdQAkuiaxHvlLksQBv4cQ70qRCyQp9N7gtIfKJ-xm9E=)******

body:
We hope you are staying safe and healthy during this difficult time for our community. We wanted to take a moment today to update you on the latest information regarding COVID-19.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16304047/173039413/1960319551?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmNhYjgwNC1iZDZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ipEZX-XjTqUhY8wbDjIn0GEWxPbQGtDYqcaay8kDnng=&emci=59daab80-ac6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6fcab804-bd6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249) Team,

We hope you are staying safe and healthy during this difficult time for our community. We wanted to take a moment today to update you on the latest information regarding COVID-19.

Since Eugene last reached out, Governor Tom Wolf has declared **a state of emergency in Pennsylvania** **** and issued an order to shutter all non-life sustaining business. It’s important to note that businesses such as hospitals, pharmacies, groceries and gas stations are considered “life sustaining” and will remain open. **** **** | ****_**Resources for Pennsylvanians:**_**** ******➜[Click here to find more information on what businesses are considered life sustaining versus non-life sustaining.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16304048/173039414/-131551214?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmNhYjgwNC1iZDZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ipEZX-XjTqUhY8wbDjIn0GEWxPbQGtDYqcaay8kDnng=&emci=59daab80-ac6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6fcab804-bd6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249)

➜ [Click here to stay up-to-date on information from the Pennsylvania Department of Health.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16304049/173039415/-961547894?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmNhYjgwNC1iZDZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ipEZX-XjTqUhY8wbDjIn0GEWxPbQGtDYqcaay8kDnng=&emci=59daab80-ac6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6fcab804-bd6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249)

➜ The PA 211 hotline is available to those in need during this challenging time. Call 211 or text your zip code to #898-211 to talk with a resource specialist for free.****** **** ****
---
**** **** As a team, we have been carefully heeding the advice of [**the CDC**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16304050/173039416/2080337457?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NTE3NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZmNhYjgwNC1iZDZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ipEZX-XjTqUhY8wbDjIn0GEWxPbQGtDYqcaay8kDnng=&emci=59daab80-ac6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6fcab804-bd6a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=10203249) and practicing social distancing. While we do not know how long this crisis will continue, we know that the best thing anyone can do right now to slow the spread of this virus and ease the burden on our health care system is to stay home, if you are able.

Team, we might not be able to gather in person, but we want you to know that you are not alone. This is an unprecedented situation we find ourselves in. Now, more than ever, we need to come together to support and uplift each other.

We will continue to check in with you to provide information and updates as it arrives.

_\--Everyone on Team Eugene_ **** ****
**** ****

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: Peter, we can stand tall during this crisis and help people in your hometown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
---
Peter,

This is certainly a difficult time for so many. Between workers being laid off, schools closing, and shortages of groceries and other items, **folks are struggling right now.**

When times get tough, it’s important that we look out for our friends and neighbors -- especially our older neighbors who might be scared to head outside to the grocery store.

**In the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, Feeding America is collecting monetary donations to keep their food banks running strong for all of the folks who rely on them for nutritious meals.**

**[Now more than ever, we need to support our communities. Can you extend some generosity by making a donation to Feeding America today?](https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true)**

The Feeding America network of food banks and programs serve 40 million people across the country, including 12 million kids and 7 million seniors. **That number is sure to go up during these times of crisis.**

While we are practicing social distancing and leaving our homes only when necessary, we can’t help out in all of the ways we normally would -- but we can still make a difference.

**[As costs are rapidly mounting because of increased demand, can you help out Feeding America with a contribution today?](https://secure.feedingamerica.org/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app20122b?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16307061/173064512/-2075069224?28371.donation=form1&idb=155356515&df_id=28371&mfc_pref=T&NONCE_TOKEN=1F90F54D58D3F6FF47E2C9F325DF264D&s_onsite_promo=hp_slider&s_subsrc=https://www.feedingamerica.org/about-us/press-room&28371_donation=form1&s_src=W203ORGSC&s_referrer=google&_ga=2.184547238.1038545703.1584545003-1176301857.1584545003&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWFjYzQ1MC1jNTZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W9-HFPjFEqIkGrMpZL_IlSl5lPAqwOgFi6AXEdE3WVg=&emci=9f132524-f469-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=09acc450-c56a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=6658013)

clean_body: Peter, This is certainly a difficult time for so many. Between workers being laid off, schools closing, and shortages of groceries and other items, folks are struggling right now. When times get tough, it’s important that we look out for our friends and neighbors -- especially our older neighbors who might be scared to head outside to the grocery store. In the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, Feeding America is collecting monetary donations to keep their food banks running strong for all of the folks who rely on them for nutritious meals. The Feeding America network of food banks and programs serve 40 million people across the country, including 12 million kids and 7 million seniors. That number is sure to go up during these times of crisis. While we are practicing social distancing and leaving our homes only when necessary, we can’t help out in all of the ways we normally would -- but we can still make a difference. Thank you so much for helping out. I’m constantly surprised by the generosity and kindness of Americans -- and I know that during this time of fear and uncertainty it will be no different. Standing Tall for All, J.D. Scholten

body:
I hope that you and your loved ones are doing OK right now. There is a lot of uncertainty in the world, and a lot of people in our community are struggling right now. | | | [
](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h0/hhRpZZW1RfrqdNP9De00vQRlUIk-2BKhU3wA9r6CKglNIxlrbVX39UzVilwU7sMdOTF5nJNHMs72NsgL7qJuloKLbKEEsFzbP9jfEBIkmmfriejW2FaUlhcb8YL4Wt-2Bu0se0UFF-2FcehXC9AnNEvDY-2FO-2Bimf70-2B-2B7qp3099b9RobYqAc7mSLyhX7A78DU33bOs9ZSatL-2FBRdB5-2Fb5o63bWr-2FUwoDeIQZECnh-2BTzMzuV3P60NRE4Fl55Fy4N0-2B-2BMub1PlrTXF8NUpA7QqkOoanJcGA-3D-3D/GPWI) Friend, **** **** **** I hope that you and your loved ones are doing OK right now. There is a lot of uncertainty in the world, and a lot of people in our community are struggling. As disorienting as things can be, I am inspired by everyone who is stepping up to support those in need. Today, I want to share some ways that you can help those who need a hand.
Here in New York’s 17th Congressional District, we have some great organizations dedicated to feeding those in need. **[Feeding Westchester](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h1/j7wDDsEz59IWb3moJj-2F5gChQXR2jslx-2BR2egu570dIcmyhZRIS-2BMIKgI3A3wJeCVvubuDN5VfFZf9D9NkmSijS0s0BPybYeuTSf9xAEa4esGwAgHiYRTbQoIJmAcgrmPbRYAgHu5tSMwSApEapt79QyRdihHMbUwJSu8YqgkLYLvEihYjaDDHmtYmw5g3oWS5S6EakpeeneLU26dUeJsyxAmbuZX9lZSxaCii7ubRHX-2BJhEm65TSEIe4PqSy51ehX4pnkEOhB2smQMOGSXMRMg-3D-3D/aKmD)** and **[Rockland Community Against Hunger](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h2/nagD1Sb8xpM2CGHJS2Jm59u5tyk4Qqjuq36oXP0pa1iX9dWeHQMYSLn-2FKfmHALeEp0Oe-2Bn-2FN-2BIUqSZgEIq7qLvixVcuirFEdEY01fc5G6GhSCyPJ0zQGS3bCUVR29fgYCh44GZad6oM-2FFsFhlysxZGqSA8N3tewEavetx9L-2Bka2neEppo1D5JJ8cbSaic92IJDadElfdU1KCDgi1KkMBEJe8TucRZeVhXssl8vCvJuxevt-2BgUlm3wCU2IPicdG-2FG9Z-2BzIHpFaogKq0Cpd7b1NA-3D-3D/NltF)** are both great places to support right now. With many in our community losing shifts or being laid off, let’s make sure people can still put food on the table for their families. While all donations are appreciated, if you can, please donate money instead of food. Food banks can use cash donations to purchase food in bulk much more efficiently than accepting food donations.
We are also experiencing an extreme blood shortage due to the cancellation of thousands of blood drives across the country. If you are healthy and able to do so, **[please use this link to make an appointment to donate blood](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h3/J21XlquhpKmgOv1sS7dyi8I6eFjEhXxGe1ysRU0OamE7ivhgYIvPD4dDApzIYZXw4gogZXOl0nUaDinhM6Jo8SOVDr1GxsEj5ZQg-2FoWn-2FSFmyq-2Bdnx45WAsj-2BHrHcN7OTrQ9s2kG5rM5ifGzuNrPEfA1A-2FK4bUzSgqwWkKl2mEJ6bOXb1wLHX4UaPxL9bN-2FtAyQl8v9lTLR4codQz5iIn6wLOSXy7yME2wFr8bH6zGVRPvypYauWkvlbUegkZ-2FurRimh6h17T6whmOize2eQaYs5y-2Bo0chzbbOxVZOOFdck-3D/NXDB)**.
One of the most at-risk populations right now are immigrants in detention facilities, where close living conditions make them particularly vulnerable to the virus. The Brooklyn Community Bail Fund has **[organized a New York Immigrant Freedom Fund where you can donate to pay immigration bond](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h4/11IGMRaX1JeiNtv5Ga-2F4Bzdmjcb8xzLiaudefG9Cr1LDcaL9WbA2TapwILFIw2cS52jUL9U7TnpEL-2FGvPPERmH26HrrIWwcdlfuw4Y8hPnOPY92ZeLOa-2BzfHUYe-2FjSZuK-2FSFNc9rjocLzGVAye7WK9zEEW0d9C99lLoe4kiNnBm3n9WBHSpFlLBMm-2FKkasrkJBShI3dXX43hwxLQzOES-2FZtsrX-2FCOLpE-2F-2FkRBjEgW-2FmPSaBRH75NBpH6Bp9RuQEvQwS9-2ByPv0DG8fCpWe110XVDqb3THhMl2RmipPPaJwHU-3D/Fzic)** for community members who can’t afford it.
There are other small things you can do within your immediate networks to support your friends and family. If you know people who live alone, especially who are older, please call them and check-in to see how they’re doing and if they need a meal or anything else right now. Even just a phone conversation can go a long way in helping someone who is lacking their usual human contact.
Please also consider shopping at local restaurants that are currently offering take-out and delivery. These businesses are the heart and soul of our communities, and this crisis has hit restaurants and restaurant workers particularly hard. Shout out to a few personal favorites, **[Black Cat Cafe in Irvington](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h5/OXlLK9ioXIi7yPFEdFEJss4VX20dAOo6xU8mW0sNiXaZbnmnnZPq-2FP19A-2B3eckY35OxhnczX-2FKO82kC7seKoSEuJuOAMQk5Dxu0YKk7k7QqPWuGXHkH8J8Dg9rbnC-2B7S97M1b3nm4fVP2Ut1go7FlM6381MXr-2BZDOnZf9Pi3iABZbGB7TiJlnZQdhjzG62gyTRKh-2B19qugwGszYPtO0EpdXIEbaxNPVkWsoSOCAzCSAT95n4ompUbM9KJs2yQuSP/aexi)** , which offers curbside pick up, and **[Amici Italian Restaurant& Pizzeria in Nyack](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h6/VhVLbAiqG1VvEpXqaVQZHBTIWED0gkBoiOgjBEARvfeygkAlqLj9S5Wbn3o4HyAwy1HLUTb-2BIFVZLgZ199sheaV4zWEL5yeBgwxgEnbUlbX8GlGtnKf67k9V9ZGxisWtXfZAkyyrKHGHUbbonRhFLKZj9Lc-2Bgz5G-2F7W8Nx1F3cYUUESAmdDgauNheFeXlXiptc2eGoqAKvrY0dznfK8F-2BAKzl3S2-2Fk084i-2FsirvYQMh4724vUEjxll9uxuliZwXl/PKfj)**. And if you order delivery, please remember to tip well!
If you have more ideas of ways to help, or charities that the campaign can amplify, please let us know. These are uncertain times, but together we can get through anything.
In solidarity, Mondaire [Facebook](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h7/Nejq3qQQ6Jn1R8HM5bnp8AsJlRvyv3gSneUqeLKLznBCoz42UjHRg-2F9mAL1dFJ3ZOqSRdiG3mszt01x7QsJX2qcrBV0s-2Fz134L9oIdCOWbRZLDc3gjHS5T7mc6UwEmziCWRs4-2Fuoul5OM0sqSvpHsL7-2FTdaB-2FIPhgYeJYGavv-2ByBw2guMwKLMtSDQsGwPzcsaa1JRK11fjGurLtzF566RtyjOO0Lzx-2B7OtJqU3gxfoX1yM1YXdGPiONpENyM5WJ8-2FMgWDL4JpiBVxAY1P60zxQ-3D-3D/TnMP) | [Twitter](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h8/tX6tdXlLuYnKkE2jZWciQUs1NelnZQ77h2lAz4D56MJUry7-2BROcNsln479TXBeretFI-2BJtYecW6kYTs1FvrQ4YygXLpDlwdzErAKbzKrkdwWDyjlTLKMUyfN379qV4jUuV7Cj7fLfAyEsOm13Zvi00tqHon05JBdqGVvnXjpBOLIhzQkB-2FvuTiGOsApiYWD6JDw6r0mXD8D0H4ZgH1-2BT1V0Ij7RVMan0tBx4WC4kuq8jx78d4-2B1z0EqGv7vj6nN1ljHqsQtLDyDzbwabgA-2BhUw-3D-3D/19Rp) | [Instagram](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/mps2/c/HwE/ni0YAA/t.308/kJxWAtx0RxebhUSNAwca5g/h9/UgaStWBIiLYn8eevue7q8FAhcGqAoYLOwsOn0RlPD886rRcJZIsaAG91B3gZr9SeZlH2Xh6PdWBdqr1-2BBFcw5am6ISt-2FEMDpRnHb5xS2oGLFpxGDJ2-2BpjJkkbo65f1IUOrYilcgpqkN23RzobZOjoolwHIeEjlO61ZXzvs8oID1aYFDWNKaNAcmnkQXqPiu-2BPgKVV8yAaaK5d5FEu7ujTbdydC6OK0HlJxCkd-2BvxMnOPAN4Og6tZpZa8X-2BaWUUDBIUAbEZvbVHvVucLpWbqs9Q-3D-3D/rOW_)

name: Mary Mancini
subject: TNDP Weekly Recap: Democrats Fight COVID-19
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/tJLKn-4F5Ee9e7tBzzVl5A2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/tJLKn-4F5Ee9e7tBzzVl5A2]
Dear Peter ,
In the face of the Coronavirus pandemic, Democrats from Washington to Nashville are not hiding, they’re not blaming, they’re not making excuses - they are stepping up to get work done on behalf of the people of the state and country.
In Nashville, Democrats in the TN General Assembly are working across the aisle to try to pass mandatory legislation to keep the government up and running. While Republicans took it as an opportunity to pass a $40 million voucher program, here are some of the things Democrats fought for while the budget discussion carried on until Midnight Thursday:
*
Ensure
medical
treatment
for
people
who
don’t
have
health
insurance

*
Build
up
healthcare
facilities,
equipment
and
supplies

*
Provide
needed
relief
for
people
who
are
out
of
work
and
businesses
that
are
struggling

*
100%
No
Excuse
Needed
Vote
By
Mail
[https://tndp.org/petition-tennessee-legislature-pass-vote-mail-now/]

It was a heated night, and even after Republicans made it impossible to pass legislation to protect Tennesseans, Democrats never gave up. Here are a few Democratic hot takes and quotes from the night:
We’re spending $1 Million to test deer, but a human of Tennessee has to reach a certain threshold to be tested. This is a matter of values. Who do we value most: our people or deer? Do we want to be testing deer carcus or human carcus? Do we want to test Bambi or should we start testing Randy, Andy, and Sandy? - Representative Antonio Parkinson
We're supposed to be here passing a budget to address an emergency situation to the people of TN, but, ya'll are up here money grubbing. - Representative Bo Mitchell
Middle Tennessee was hit by a tornado and now the whole state by this pandemic........ We are flush with cash in this state. Are we spending it wisely to help Tennessee? - Representative John Ray Clemons
I don’t like the idea of having to vote for a budget that contains vouchers (which is the most misguided legislation I’ve ever seen.) But, my biggest concern with this budget is we’re not doing enough to address this virus and crisis. - Representative Bo Mitchell
The way we have managed this state has left too many people vulnerable in the face of this crisis. Everyone in this room knows we would be in better shape if we hadn’t closed 13 hospitals across this state. Everyone in this room knows we would be in better shape if we didn’t have hundreds of thousands of uninsured.- Senator Jeff Yarbro
We applaud every single Democrat fighting for the health and safety of people of Tennessee. A smart, effective government that keeps its people safe should be the goal of every elected representative we send to Washington and Nashville. If they can't do the work necessary to protect us through these difficult times, they need to get out of the way and let Democrats lead - it is evident now more than ever Democrats are the most equipped.
As we navigate these weird and uncertain times, we hope you'll remember your Democratic values and continue to care for yourself, your state, and your neighbor.
Standing with you from a responsible social distance,
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/signature2.png]
- Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party
TENNESSEE DEMOCRATS ARE STEPPING
UP TO LEAD IN TIME OF CRISIS
All across the state and up and down the ballot, Democrats are stepping up to lead! Just this week, 9 Democrats pulled petitions in Williamson County alone. Read more! [https://www.tennessean.com/story/news/local/williamson/2020/03/19/9-democrats-williamson-county-tn-state-house-petitions/2878305001/]
REPUBLICANS USED A NATIONAL
PANDEMIC TO SUPPRESS VOTERS
The Tennessee General Assembly had half a dozen different opportunities to pass Vote By Mail legislation while passing mandatory laws to recess session during the Coronavirus pandemic. The Republican supermajority voted it down each time, and instead voted to create voting super-sites. This is in direct conflict with CDC recommendations and force voters to choose between staying safe or casting their vote. Tennesseans should not have to choose between keeping their families safe and casting a vote. [https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=194525941989750]
https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=194525941989750 [https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=194525941989750]
5 TN REPUBLICANS VOTE AGAINST
BIPARTISAN CORONAVIRUS AID BILL
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/90070033_6175324419311_6950860297435348992_n.png.jpg]
Tennesseans are losing their jobs at an alarming rate, parents are trying to figure out how they will continue to feed their children, and the uninsured are endangering themselves and others for fear of Coronavirus testing costs; yet five Tennessee Republicans voted NO for the necessary steps that will keep Tennesseans safe, secure, and free.
They voted NO on a bipartisan Coronavirus relief package that passed the House 363-40 and the Senate 90 - 8. They voted NO on an aid package that enhances unemployment insurance, guarantees free testing, & provides up to 10 days of paid sick leave. They voted NO as Tennesseans face one of the largest crises we have ever faced.
Read the full story. [https://fox17.com/news/local/tennessee-democratic-party-issues-statement-condemning-votes-against-covid-19-aid-billc]
TNDP TO HOLD CONGRESSIONAL
CONVENTIONS REMOTELY
In response to the Coronavirus pandemic, out of an abundance of caution, and to reduce social density, promote social distancing, and in light of meeting locations canceling scheduled events, the Tennessee Democratic Party will be switching from in-person Congressional District Conventions to remote conventions using a teleconference system.
Remote congressional district convention caucuses will still be held on Saturday, March 21st. Start times will be staggered throughout the day with the first caucuses to start at 9 am central.
“The Tennessee Democratic Party is prioritizing the health of all Tenneasseans during this time. It is during times like these our commitment to democracy and all people is so vital. We stand with Tennesseans every step of the way as this situation develops.” - Mary Mancini, TNDP Chair
IF YOU DON'T KNOW, NOW YOU KNOW:
A TNDP PODCAST
https://tndp.org/podcast/ [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
This year we head to the ballot box, both locally and nationally. It feels weird to say that given teh current state of our country and world, but, elections now are crucial to our economy and health. Use some of your time at home to catch up on some of the biggest stories in Tennessee's legislature.
If You Don’t Know, Now You Know: A TNDP Podcast is hosted by Tennessee Democratic Party Chair, Mary Mancini. Recorded live from the Tennessee state capitol in Nashville, we speak with lawmakers, activists, and candidates, people who can tell you – and show you – how the laws are made that affect our lives. There’s no shortage of issues to be covered – about freedom, opportunity, equity, justice, and safety.
Listen now! [https://tndp.org/podcast/]
TWEETS OF THE WEEK
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-20%20at%2010.31.58%20AM.png]
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/77134/images/Screen%20Shot%202020-03-20%20at%2010.33.53%20AM.png]
YOUR CONTRIBUTIONS IN ACTION
In the face of the pandemic, Democrats are leading the charge to save and protect Tennesseeans. Before they were elected though, they had to be recruited and trained through the Democratic Party. Any contribution you give today, will help us strengthen our pipeline of candidates and ensure we have effective campaigns running.
Will you consider donating today? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/oNKhpMgsTk-zA4pXE2fn4w2]
PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR. GEETA MCMILLAN, TREASURER NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. CONTRIBUTIONS OR GIFTS TO TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY ARE NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE.

clean_body: [] Dear Peter , In the face of the Coronavirus pandemic, Democrats from Washington to Nashville are not hiding, they’re not blaming, they’re not making excuses - they are stepping up to get work done on behalf of the people of the state and country. In Nashville, Democrats in the TN General Assembly are working across the aisle to try to pass mandatory legislation to keep the government up and running. While Republicans took it as an opportunity to pass a $40 million voucher program, here are some of the things Democrats fought for while the budget discussion carried on until Midnight Thursday: Ensure medical treatment for people who don’t have health insurance Build up healthcare facilities, equipment and supplies Provide needed relief for people who are out of work and businesses that are struggling 100% No Excuse Needed Vote By Mail [] It was a heated night, and even after Republicans made it impossible to pass legislation to protect Tennesseans, Democrats never gave up. Here are a few Democratic hot takes and quotes from the night: We’re spending $1 Million to test deer, but a human of Tennessee has to reach a certain threshold to be tested. This is a matter of values. Who do we value most: our people or deer? Do we want to be testing deer carcus or human carcus? Do we want to test Bambi or should we start testing Randy, Andy, and Sandy? - Representative Antonio Parkinson We're supposed to be here passing a budget to address an emergency situation to the people of TN, but, ya'll are up here money grubbing. - Representative Bo Mitchell Middle Tennessee was hit by a tornado and now the whole state by this pandemic........ We are flush with cash in this state. Are we spending it wisely to help Tennessee? - Representative John Ray Clemons I don’t like the idea of having to vote for a budget that contains vouchers (which is the most misguided legislation I’ve ever seen.) But, my biggest concern with this budget is we’re not doing enough to address this virus and crisis. - Representative Bo Mitchell The way we have managed this state has left too many people vulnerable in the face of this crisis. Everyone in this room knows we would be in better shape if we hadn’t closed 13 hospitals across this state. Everyone in this room knows we would be in better shape if we didn’t have hundreds of thousands of uninsured.- Senator Jeff Yarbro We applaud every single Democrat fighting for the health and safety of people of Tennessee. A smart, effective government that keeps its people safe should be the goal of every elected representative we send to Washington and Nashville. If they can't do the work necessary to protect us through these difficult times, they need to get out of the way and let Democrats lead - it is evident now more than ever Democrats are the most equipped. As we navigate these weird and uncertain times, we hope you'll remember your Democratic values and continue to care for yourself, your state, and your neighbor. Standing with you from a responsible social distance, [] - Mary Mancini Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party TENNESSEE DEMOCRATS ARE STEPPING UP TO LEAD IN TIME OF CRISIS All across the state and up and down the ballot, Democrats are stepping up to lead! Just this week, 9 Democrats pulled petitions in Williamson County alone. Read more! [] REPUBLICANS USED A NATIONAL PANDEMIC TO SUPPRESS VOTERS The Tennessee General Assembly had half a dozen different opportunities to pass Vote By Mail legislation while passing mandatory laws to recess session during the Coronavirus pandemic. The Republican supermajority voted it down each time, and instead voted to create voting super-sites. This is in direct conflict with CDC recommendations and force voters to choose between staying safe or casting their vote. Tennesseans should not have to choose between keeping their families safe and casting a vote. [] [] 5 TN REPUBLICANS VOTE AGAINST BIPARTISAN CORONAVIRUS AID BILL [] Tennesseans are losing their jobs at an alarming rate, parents are trying to figure out how they will continue to feed their children, and the uninsured are endangering themselves and others for fear of Coronavirus testing costs; yet five Tennessee Republicans voted NO for the necessary steps that will keep Tennesseans safe, secure, and free. They voted NO on a bipartisan Coronavirus relief package that passed the House 363-40 and the Senate 90 - 8. They voted NO on an aid package that enhances unemployment insurance, guarantees free testing, & provides up to 10 days of paid sick leave. They voted NO as Tennesseans face one of the largest crises we have ever faced. Read the full story. name: Team Michelle
subject: Here's why Elaine is on Team Michelle:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR KANSAS
body:
Being able to travel the district and talk with supporters has been one of Mayor Michelle De La Isla's favorite parts of running this campaign. Throughout our travels, we've been fortunate to meet so many incredible Kansans and hear their stories.

Friends,

Being able to travel the district and talk with supporters has been one of Mayor Michelle De La Isla's favorite parts of running this campaign. Throughout our travels, we've been fortunate to meet so many incredible Kansans and hear their stories.

Across the district, people are standing with Michelle because they know she's the leader we need to lift up our voices and fight for our Kansas values in the U.S. Congress. And since we're unable to meet with people face-to-face for the time being, we wanted to take a moment to share some of the stories we've heard from everyday Kansans who have joined us in this fight.

Here's what Elaine from Baxter Springs had to say about why she's supporting Michelle:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16315290/173141408/-1025822652?gpsid=8_122_12173&refcode=em20_200320_story_fnd_gq_ful__tm_ettx&utm_campaign=em20_200320_story_fnd_gq_ful__tm_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=mdli&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTUzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmNhMDAzZC1lMTZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-oZJbFL4tF_sTdMRd-758bDbg357x3e-xBdZ6RcVqk=) _There have been candidates that come and go through the political seasons, but you know when that special person steps into the arena with conviction, compassion and strength. That's what I see in Michelle De La Isla. She's my kind of person and possesses the values, character and grit that I'm proud to support.

As I learn more about Michelle's life and her story, there is a respect for her transparency and truth that is rare in politics today. She is a true servant, and has a selfless concern and love for people as shown by her words and actions. I am so excited to see what the future holds for Michelle, and for the great work she will do for her fellow Kansans and our country!

Join me in supporting Michelle for Congress!

\-- Elaine, Baxter Springs, KS_

We're so grateful to have all of you on our team, and we look forward to when we can get back on the road and meet you face-to-face to hear your stories, and stand up for Kansans together.

Thanks,

Team Michelle

****[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16315292/173141412/-1025822652?gpsid=8_122_12173&refcode=em20_200320_story_fnd_gq_ful__tm_ettx&utm_campaign=em20_200320_story_fnd_gq_ful__tm_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=mdli&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTUzOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmNhMDAzZC1lMTZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=d-oZJbFL4tF_sTdMRd-758bDbg357x3e-xBdZ6RcVqk=)****
---

PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR KANSAS
Michelle for Kansas
PO Box 67101
Topeka, KS 66667

name: Tina Smith
subject: Second Harvest Heartland
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Friends — As the coronavirus continues to spread, so many families are facing immeasurable hardships. On top of trying to stay safe and healthy, folks across the country are wondering how they’re going to put food on the table as more and more people lose their jobs or get sent home without pay. **It’s an untenable position to be in, but I want you to know that I’m with you. I understand what you’re going through and I’m working my tail off in the Senate to make sure families like yours receive the assistance they deserve right now.** In the meantime, **Second Harvest Heartland** is working their tails off, too. They’re one of the nation’s largest hunger-relief organizations fighting around the clock to ensure no Minnesota family goes hungry during this crisis. If there’s anything I know about this incredible grassroots team, it's our willingness to lend a helping hand — especially when it matters most. And I’m emailing you now because _this is when it matters most._ **[If you have anything to spare, can I count on you to add a donation to Second Harvest Heartland today? Even just a few bucks will make an immediate difference in someone’s life. I can’t stress enough how important this is.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16313785/173126346/-63012667?refcode=em200320-secondharvest&amounts=10,25,50,100,150,250,1000&firstname=&lastname=&email=&addr1=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzkwZGE2Yy1kYzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4jf6H06i-d4J5Dy11IJRAQr6PuuCYwRujllaqOmDNSI=)** | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16313787/173126348/-63012667?refcode=em200320-secondharvest&amounts=10,25,50,100,150,250,1000&firstname=&lastname=&email=&addr1=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YzkwZGE2Yy1kYzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4jf6H06i-d4J5Dy11IJRAQr6PuuCYwRujllaqOmDNSI=)
---
Please, only give if you can afford to do so. I know these are troubling and uncertain times for many of us, so if your family is facing financial hardship, it’s okay to sit this one out. I appreciate you opening this email more than you’ll ever know.
Thank you — and look out for yourself and your loved ones.
Tina
Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
---
Tina Smith for Minnesota
PO Box 14362
St. Paul, MN 55114
United States

Tina Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota Tina Smith for Minnesota PO Box 14362 St. Paul, MN 55114 United States Contributions or gifts to Tina Smith for Minnesota are not tax-deductible.

name: MJ Hegar
subject: A clear path
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Now more than ever, we're relying on online donations to keep our campaign's momentum going.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489021f/7f51f7bc/4c115b2e/4ef1e4d1/50032246/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Hey Peter, I'm reaching out today because you're a critical part of the team that's going to help us win this election.

**Now more than ever, we 're relying on online donations to keep our campaign's momentum going.** As we adapt to a new campaign landscape, recurring online donations will give us the resources to budget for ads, staffing needs, and our texting and phonebanking programs.

**[Will you start a recurring donation to help give our campaign a clear path forward?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489021f/7f51f7bc/4c115b2e/4ef1e4d0/50032246/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTIifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yrrecurring?refcode=email_20200319.2nd__1092__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

We make critical budget decisions based on expected fundraising, so **recurring grassroots donations** help us make important investments.

Meanwhile, Sen. Cornyn is propped up by **big pharma, the NRA, and corporate PACs,** so he doesn't have to rely on grassroots donors. Instead, his corporate PACs make sure he has all the money he needs to purchase his ads. Your recurring donation helps us fight back against Sen. Cornyn's big money.

**[If you're able, will you commit to a monthly recurring donation of any amount that you can fit into your monthly budget?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489021f/7f51f7bc/4c115b2e/4ef1e4d0/50032246/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTIifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yrrecurring?refcode=email_20200319.2nd__1092__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

It all adds up to make a BIG difference -- thank you for all of your support. Together, we're going to make a big difference for Texas.

\-- MJ

**[**DONATE**](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489021f/7f51f7bc/4c115b2e/4ef1e4d0/50032246/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwOTIifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yrrecurring?refcode=email_20200319.2nd__1092__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

name: Team Messner
subject: This has been a difficult week - but we will get through this!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MESSNER FOR SENATE
body:
Tap or click to read more >>
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/0ffebce745127ef6c3d6b838967d376c/33153) [ Contact Us ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/438b4f49183f5601c3d6b838967d376c/33153) Entrepreneur. Veteran. Conservative. | |
---
**We’re All in This Together!**
The reality of the coronavirus and the precautions put in place have impacted our daily lives here in New Hampshire. In this week’s newsletter, we’re not only updating you about the campaign and how we’ve adapted to social distancing guidelines, but providing useful information on Coronavirus-related resources.

We hope the links we’ve included are helpful as you, your family and friends, and your communities deal with an unprecedented change in our social landscape.

We are a proud state, with people of independent spirit and a caring nature. We will get through this, together!
|
---
**Corky Transitions “Conversations” Tour into a New Initiative**
During the next week and a half, Corky Messner will be using his announced “Granite State Conversations” platform to listen to New Hampshire residents, support small businesses, and show appreciation to those on the front lines fighting COVID-19.

One part of that platform was a telephone Town Hall on March 19th.
_Corky grabbed take-out from a popular Manchester restaurant this week._
Corky was joined by 400+ Granite Staters, who discussed the coronavirus response as well as other issues critical to New Hampshire.

In the days to come, Corky will continue to focus on methods to safely interact with voters during this pandemic, such as virtual town halls and advocating for ways for Granite Staters to support local businesses and first responders. The campaign will always be mindful of social distancing recommendations from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and other health agencies. Corky grabbed take-out from a popular Manchester restaurant this week. As Corky explains, “We are all in this together."
Our state and federal governments, first responders, and medical professionals are rightfully focusing their efforts on defeating this disease. We stand by them in their efforts. However, it is also important to ensure that we fully understand the significant financial and social impact that the current precautions and restrictions are having on our schools, small businesses, and communities. I want to hear from these individuals and I want to be their advocates as we navigate through this crisis.

“From purchasing gift cards to ordering ‘take-out,’ there are many ways we can help our local restaurants and small businesses,” says Corky. “We must come together to donate and find ways to support our first responders and medical professionals who are on the frontline fighting this disease. I look forward to doing my part to help.
|
---
**Need More Information?
**
We’ve pulled together some links to provide information you need to keep yourself, your family and friends, and your community healthy in light of this pandemic. Please review the info below, and do your part to help our state and our nation deal with this public health crisis.

Remember, the following is only a small sampling of the resources available.
**HEALTH INFORMATON**

White House Guidelines: 15 Days to Slow the Spread – Click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/5fea461ce9baeb49c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
New Hampshire Department of Health & Human Services Fact Sheet – Click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/c5ce2c19ef196f5dc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
New Hampshire Division of Public Health Services – Click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/540f25887a9a65a0c3d6b838967d376c/33153).**
For general assistance, a 211 site to locate resources – Click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/64668350c6ea9d48c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
Centers for Disease Control: What to do if you are sick with coronavirus disease – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/7ba7c3824b5f68fcc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.

**RESOURCES FOR BUSINESSES/EMPLOYMENT**

**NH Department of Employment Security**
Due to COVID-19, all claims and questions should be directed to nhes.nh.gov or call the Unemployment Assistance Hotline at
603-271-7700.

**Coronavirus (COVID-19): Small Business Guidance & Loan Resources** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/ede30e3b077f1d68c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**Resources for Small Business** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/6fd5f1ab64afb7c8c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.

**RESOURCES FOR PARENTS AND SCHOOLS**

**NH Department of Education** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/3cefc87de5afaba5c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**How to Home School Your Children** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/5849b8afe8473c41c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.

**FOR ASSISTANCE – FOOD**

**Meals on Wheels** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/561e7265f8c11b6bc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**NH Food Ban** k – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/a2f2cdea0a508598c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**School Food and Nutrition Service** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/dd4dc6bb120832bdc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
|
---
**How You Can Help**
Many support agencies in New Hampshire need your help. Please consider giving back to your community – donate blood, contact the local food bank to see what they might need, or just visit a neighbor who lives alone and needs assistance. Here are a few helpful sites.

**NH Food Bank** – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/02cddbd1ed6f48dfc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**American Red Cross** – New Hampshire – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/01643be552a83706c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
**Meals on Wheels** – To volunteer – click **[here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/1ee1a3b3c2e258c5c3d6b838967d376c/33153)**.
|
---
**Last Word: Supporting Each Other**
In a recent Messner Minute, Corky talks about the importance of supporting local businesses and others impacted by the coronavirus pandemic guidelines. **[Listen in](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/3410277eeef5b11dc3d6b838967d376c/33153)**!
[ Donate Now! ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/33b4322786ae4dedc3d6b838967d376c/33153)
|
---
Corky Messner for Senate
[Email us](mailto:info@corkyforsenate.com)
[Visit our website](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/cc1d33a50c62df3e322761119fed00b9/33153/66d8a257e57ca1ec/fd19b7260a1d2571c3d6b838967d376c/33153)

| A Message from Matt!
---
For many of us around NH, this has been a very trying week. We are entering an unprecedented time in our his state's history, but I know that we are going to come out stronger than ever before! While my campaign is still very much moving at full steam, we temporarily have shifted our focus away from public events to social media and technology based campaigning. Please stay tuned to our Facebook and Twitter accounts for more details.

| |
---
| This week, we introduced our list of 8 things that the Federal Government can do _right now_ to help the average American during the pandemic. Please take a look at our video below and share it with friends and neighbors. I hope that you all remain safe, calm and watch out for each other in the coming days and weeks. Thank you. \- Matt

| | | **One final thought**

| |

|

| This past Sunday President Trump asked for a National Day of Prayer, and I would like to share with you the prayer that I shared on my Facebook page and that brings me comfort during these anxious times:
---
| |
---
| |
---
"Lord, come into my life today, calm the anxiety, relieve the pressure of the outside world around us, heal those who are sick, protect those who are the healers, wrap your arms around a world that in unsure and afraid. Lord, protect me, my family and friends and bring a calm and patience to those around me. Help me to be a better person and to help those less fortunate than myself. Forgive my sins. Start my day fresh and new with your love filling the voids and calming the anxieties in my life. Come into my life and make me whole. Thank you. Amen"

name: Andrew Yang
subject: It’s Time to Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Humanity Forward
body:
|
---

| | Hello,

Thank you for your support! Our ideas are about to be implemented – and just in the nick of time.

_The Atlantic_ , _The Washington Post_ , POLITICO, and _The New York Times_ have all interviewed me in the past 2 days about the fact that Universal Basic Income is about to have its biggest rollout in the history of the United States. It’s great that our government may do the right thing.

But we know that the suffering in our communities is happening RIGHT NOW. People are desperate. And we have to help.

I am very happy to announce that Humanity Forward is going to be giving $1 million to working poor families in New York and around the country immediately. The money will go to working poor families in the Bronx, tipped and wage workers in New York State who have lost their jobs, and other families across the country.

Here is how you can act.

If you are in position to donate, please [**donate now**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317450/173158183/-1709120280?refcode=HUM20200320.30DO1&refcode2=jhnHONllGFMlbqV7dZcI&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=). 100% of your donation will go to someone in need.

If you need help, please [**sign up here**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317451/173158184/-151165463?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=&emci=d22c7f12-d56a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6a97028a-e76a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=919990). We are giving people micro-grants of $250 - $500 each with very little bureaucracy. Just give us your info, someone will give you a call, and then hopefully we will send you the money. No promises, as there is no way we will get to everyone. But we will be giving away every dime we raise + at least $100,000 directly through Humanity Forward’s website.

Last, [**we need volunteers**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317452/173158185/351715900?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=&emci=d22c7f12-d56a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6a97028a-e76a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=919990) to help us vet people who need the money. There are going to be a lot of phone calls to make.

Thank you for believing in me and this movement. We have already made an enormous difference in the future of our country. Now let’s take care of our people.

It’s time for us to help each other.

\- Andrew | | **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317453/173158186/-1709120280?refcode=HUM20200320.30DO1&refcode2=jhnHONllGFMlbqV7dZcI&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=)**
---
| | | If you'd like your contribution to be tax-exempt, we've partnered with The Collective Education Fund, and you can make your donation **[here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317454/173158187/698131911?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=).**

Please see [**here**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16317455/173158188/-1913313826?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjMvMS83NDAwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YTk3MDI4YS1lNzZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=H4sHzsJddr7_IpXftsjrDq6EC3RlK6VFiJnCRthptgg=&emci=d22c7f12-d56a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&emdi=6a97028a-e76a-ea11-a94c-00155d03b5dd&ceid=919990) for rules and regulations of the raffle or to enter the raffle without making a donation.

The money will go to working poor families in the Bronx, tipped and wage workers in New York State who have lost their jobs, and other families across the country. Here is how you can act. If you are in position to donate, please =. 100% of your donation will go to someone in need. If you need help, please . We are giving people micro-grants of $250 - $500 each with very little bureaucracy. Just give us your info, someone will give you a call, and then hopefully we will send you the money. No promises, as there is no way we will get to everyone. But we will be giving away every dime we raise + at least $100,000 directly through Humanity Forward’s website. Last, to help us vet people who need the money. There are going to be a lot of phone calls to make. Thank you for believing in me and this movement. We have already made an enormous difference in the future of our country. Now let’s take care of our people. It’s time for us to help each other. \- Andrew | | = | | | If you'd like your contribution to be tax-exempt, we've partnered with The Collective Education Fund, and you can make your donation =. | | | Paid for by Humanity Forward Humanity Forward P.O. Box 214 Midtown Station New York, NY 10018 United States | | If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please . Please see for rules and regulations of the raffle or to enter the raffle without making a donation.

name: Charles Booker
subject: Help families in need who have been especially impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
Even though we may be physically distant from each other, it’s more important than ever we maintain our sense of community and rely on each other for support.
#
I hope you and your family are healthy and well.
Many of us are practicing social distancing and staying at home as much as possible — I know it’s been tough adjusting to this new lifestyle, but we’re all in this together.
Even though we may be physically distant from each other, it’s more important than ever we maintain our sense of community and rely on each other for support.
[**If you can afford to help, will you chip in $5 to help families in need here in Kentucky? Your donation will be split between some of the top charities working to lead COVID-19 relief efforts.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16319226/173176369/387399153?refcode=em20200320&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWZmZGM0OS1lZjZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HrhVml3QsS0xOGYIebR0RHDHouV91taiD6EaWxPMB_g=)
**[donate to covid-19 relief efforts](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16319227/173176370/387399153?refcode=em20200320&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWZmZGM0OS1lZjZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HrhVml3QsS0xOGYIebR0RHDHouV91taiD6EaWxPMB_g=)**
Even before the pandemic, thousands of families here in our communities have been struggling to make ends meet.
It’s why we started this movement to defeat Mitch and elect a candidate who understands firsthand the challenges working-class families face.
Now that the pandemic has spread across our country, it has created even more financial challenges for our community and has exposed problems in the institutions that are supposed to support us in moments of crisis.
It’s inspired me to fight harder than ever for change this election.
[**But today, I want to ask you if you’re able to chip in $5 to help families in need who have been especially impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic. Kindness and generosity is so important in times like this.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/16319228/173176371/387399153?refcode=em20200320&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83ODg4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWZmZGM0OS1lZjZhLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2I1ZGQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HrhVml3QsS0xOGYIebR0RHDHouV91taiD6EaWxPMB_g=)
Doing this together,
Charles

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Checking in from El Paso
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Powered By People

I hope you and your family are good. That’s the most important thing.

I also hope you are doing everything you can to make sure that others are
good too. That means keeping your distance, staying away from other
people, and doing what you can to help those who are unable to help
themselves.

We’ve been hunkered down in our home for most of the last week. To get
some exercise and time outdoors, Amy has organized daily family hikes in
the Franklin mountains. Yesterday we rode our bikes down to the track at
El Paso High to race each other in the 400 meters (Ulysses won) and
compete in the long jump (Ulysses again). We’ve also been watching movies
at home (Lord of the Rings on Netflix), reading, playing board games and
catching up by phone with friends.

We’ve been checking in on older neighbors and family members to see if we
can pick them up food or groceries. So far no takers. My mom is stubborn.

First week of full-on social distancing has been OK for us, balancing
outdoors and indoors, sometimes getting on each other’s nerves and
sometimes cracking each other up. But we’re very lucky to be safe, healthy
and to have each other. Important to remember that.

Our friend Gwen works with the local food bank and let us know that they
are having a hard time meeting their volunteer needs. So yesterday we all
went down to El Pasoans Fighting Hunger and helped pack boxes for
distribution to the elderly, necessary now that the local senior citizens
centers are closed. We tried to maintain six feet distance from other
volunteers, wore gloves and washed hands again anytime we touched our face
or our phones. We packed boxes full of rice, flour, canned fish, beans,
canned pears and nuts. We competed to see how many we could get done in an
hour and then tried to beat that record the next hour. We also got to see
other people (at a distance) for the first time in days. It was the best
thing we’ve done since we’ve been isolating ourselves from others and
we’re going to volunteer again soon. There’s a good chance your local food
bank could use your help, call them to find out.

A lot of you have been getting in touch to share your stories about what
you’ve been up to this past week and what you’re doing to get prepared for
the weeks to come. Homeschooling, hunting for toilet paper (Cynthia hasn’t
been able to find any in El Paso but has an Amazon order coming in this
April), and figuring out how you’re going to make ends meet.

Some are working in hospitals and clinics, like my sister Charlotte (El
Paso), Amy’s sister Christina (New York), and our friends Debjeet (D.C.
area) and Rob (Seattle). You’ve described to me what it’s like to be on
the frontlines of this, without having the necessary tests, protective
gear and equipment. It’s been inspiring to see your determination to take
care of the people in your community, despite these serious challenges.
And it reminds us that we have to keep up the pressure on our government
to deliver — there’s no excuse for this kind of failure to test those who
are sick and protect those who treat them.

Others have shared stories of sick family members. A friend from Lubbock
wrote:

“My mom is 67 has a severe immune disease, a 101 degree fever and been
coughing her lungs up. She went to the doctor in Lubbock today (lives in
Crosbyton) and they sent her home with an antibiotic and no test!!!!
Everyone says they don’t have them.”

Another friend whose restaurant we’ve been to in Austin told me he’s
having to close for at least a month and is frustrated with the lack of
state and federal leadership that has left so many who live paycheck to
paycheck, including his employees, with nothing. I am hopeful that
whatever comprehensive federal stimulus program that is finally signed
into law will take into account those in the food service and hospitality
industries, as well as so many other affected small businesses and
employees.

These are not easy times, but we will get through them. Please continue to
be smart — keep your distance from other people and avoid gatherings — and
please keep your spirits up. Find a way to volunteer, help, or donate to
organizations and people in need. As my friend Noah says, “it’s hard to be
sad when you’re being useful.”

My best to you and your family —

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1001&akid=82373%2E444211%2E5poqmR

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
