* If you're not experiencing respiratory symptoms, take a walk outside.

**Fourth, if you want to volunteer to reach out to others who may need a connection or you want to donate to those in need,[_click here for volunteer options._](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d342/3d199a51/2f62e3d1/7364f102/4057268074/VEsHDA/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://volunteercentertriad.org/covid-19-response/")** This is an unprecedented time. My team and I have adapted our campaign to make sure we are keeping everyone safe by closing our field office, working remotely, and cancelling all our in-person events and fundraisers for the next few months. We are reaching out to people online so we can keep in touch with our communities while keeping everyone safe. [_**If you 're interested in remote volunteer options with the campaign, please click here.**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d342/3d199a51/2f62e3d1/7364f103/4057268074/VEsEBQ/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://forms.gle/EVczjjieUYKnEW5s8") I want to express appreciation for all those who are on the frontlines of this crisis: doctors, PAs, nurses, medical personnel and hospital staff, lab technicians, first responders, pharmacists, grocery store employees, and city employees who are working in essential jobs to keep our cities running, taking care of people who are sick and making sure we can buy the supplies we need to stay safe and comfortable. I also want to thank those volunteers who are packing and delivering food to school kids and the elderly and those who are working to make sure that the homeless and most vulnerable can stay safe. Your commitment to the health and safety of our neighbors during this time exemplifies the dedication and selflessness this country needs to pull through this tough time. We all need to do our best to avoid crowds, social distance, and show kindness toward one and other. I'll continue to share updates about community resources and volunteer opportunities via email. We will get through this if we all do our part. Stay safe, Kathy | | | [Donate](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d342/3d199a51/2f62e3d1/7364f100/4057268074/VEsEBA/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDMyMCIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0%3D/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200320?refcode=kme200320_m_other&refcode2=17426")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The latest on the Coronavirus response
body:
President Trump and the White House Coronavirus Task Force announced additional actions today as part of the ongoing, government-wide response to... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=14196b551c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 20, 2020
---
| | |
# **The latest on the Coronavirus response**

**President Trump** and the White House Coronavirus Task Force announced additional actions today as part of the ongoing, government-wide response to the global pandemic:
* To **help American families and businesses** , the Treasury Department announced that Tax Day will be moved from April 15 to July 15. No interest or penalties will be charged for filing during this extended window, but any American expecting refunds or credits may claim now to get their money sooner.

* To **minimize impact on our nation’s students** , the Department of Education is temporarily waiving all interest on federally held student loans. Secretary Betsy DeVos has also directed federal lenders to allow borrowers to suspend their student loan payments without penalty for at least the next 60 days.

* Early, decisive **travel restrictions** helped slow the spread of Coronavirus to our country. Today, Secretary of State Mike Pompeo announced that the United States and Mexico have agreed to restrict non-essential travel across their border.

* **More help is on the way**. The Trump Administration worked with Congress last week on a bipartisan deal to deliver economic relief and support for American families, which the President signed into law on Wednesday. More legislative action is expected in the coming days.

[Watch today’s full briefing with President Trump.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6c6d5eff54&e=3ee4985eea)

**Something to share** : [You make America great and proud. Thank you!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=03bf9c8140&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **WATCH: What you need to know**

**This week,** President Trump issued a nationwide call to action for every American to [help slow the spread](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b765ca315c&e=3ee4985eea) of Coronavirus.

**Top health officials** from the CDC and across the government are here to help:
* 🎬 **Together apart:**[What is social distancing?](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=38292c58b2&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 **Dr. Deborah Birx:** [Who needs to be tested?](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6255a4b167&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 **Surgeon General:**[Is it still safe to donate blood?](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1c430f3dae&e=3ee4985eea)

* 🎬 **Dr. Anthony Fauci:** [What to do if you feel sick](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=98a5e9fe84&e=3ee4985eea)

---
|
---
|

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Primary Goals
body:
If you're like many other Hoosiers and Americans, I'm sure you've had a very unique week. As we all face the global coronavirus pandemic, together...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/primary-goals?e=1acd9bb3d6)

Primary Goals

If you're like many other Hoosiers and Americans, I'm sure you've had a very unique week. As we all face the global coronavirus pandemic, together we've had to face new challenges -- whether that's an unexpected slow down at work, adapting to new social distancing guidelines, helping your kids with e-learning or so much more.

Through it all, though, we all share one primary goal -- protecting our most vulnerable Hoosiers from this virus.

And that's where Indiana's primary election comes in. With county employees, poll workers and voters in close proximity, we knew that holding the election as planned on May 5 could put our fellow Hoosiers at risk.

That's why today, I was proud to join Governor Eric Holcomb as he announced that he was taking executive action to move Indiana's primary election to June 2.

I'm proud to share that this is a bipartisan decision, as Governor Holcomb worked with Secretary of State Connie Lawson, the chairman of the Indiana Democrat Party and me to come to this solution that balances citizens' right to vote with Hoosiers' health and well-being.

So while we all go through this challenging time, we'll have an extra month until Primary Day. And with that, I look forward to witnessing a new age of creative virtual campaigning that'll be coming our way soon.

- Chairman Kyle Hupfer

PHOTO: Chairman Kyle Hupfer answers questions earlier today at the Statehouse during a press conference with Governor Holcomb announcing that Indiana's Primary Election will be pushed back to June 2.
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was

Keeping Hoosiers Safe: Coronavirus

Governor Eric Holcomb is continuing to take unprecedented actions to protect Hoosiers from the coronavirus, extend relief to Hoosier businesses and individuals, and provide stabilization for the future.

Check out some of the steps Governor Holcomb is taking below:
* All of Indiana’s K-12 public schools will remain closed through May 1, and non-public schools are also ordered closed. This date could be extended if the circumstances warrant.
* The U.S. Small Business Administration has issued an Economic Injury Disaster Loan declaration, (http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=a15eccf391d948c386dbcb0879f8fb0c&_e=fGQXAGBTt2Qcozuwf83MUnZYQgsMekpi-JnfyYg1IwrU8gvewdz3iO1nxgvt1KC4NjB8wIKYdEnDHV41rFdwu02M5BvgWv0QEZwUVb0Nb7aYIHW3a4t3RmVLw4jKwy6j-CRXLPiHByMUGWyxiIu9iysPv9L_Ihef5ASVN13cChYD30j7CLLFX-oPwgNju9dk_KNgUXu7ANDDiF9MzNoSP6n4oc1aT3NFBzty8n80rBCARc7gZlJEFLztgT7RqeNpKTWzT4CSgZ53UP3rgtuMvktSQnNl7bp2RNrdgi-zp7nMQ_tr3SIQVnYRj6FAmmHd&mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) per Governor Holcomb's request, which would provide targeted, low-interest loans to Indiana small businesses to help them overcome temporary revenue loss.
* He announced a new partnership with Indiana-based Eli Lilly and Company to expand COVID-19 texting so that more Hoosiers can receive a timely diagnosis.
* Indiana is adhearing to the CDC's guidance on mass gathering.
* Bars, nightclubs and restaurants are required to close to in-person patrons and may provide take-out and delivery services through the end of March.
* The deadline to file and pay your state income taxes will be extended from April 15 to July 15 to align with new federal dates.
* The state will interpret Indiana’s unemployment laws to the broadest extent possible to cover Hoosiers who are out of work because of COVID-19. Claims for unemployment can be made completely online.
* No residential eviction proceedings or foreclosure actions may be initiated during the public health emergency. This does not relieve the individual of obligations to pay rent or mortgage payments.
* Participants in the Healthy Indiana Plan (HIP) and the Children’s Health Insurance Program are not required to make premium payments.
* The commissioner of the Department of Insurance will request that insurers institute a 60-day moratorium on policy cancellations for non-payment of premiums. This does not suspend a policyholder’s obligation to make payments.
* Mental health professionals are permitted to practice via telemedicine.

To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make you're following Governor Holcomb on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/holcombforin?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

And to fight the spread of the coronavirus and protect yourself, remember to wash your hands frequently and to continue to practice social distancing. In order to flatten the curve and protect vulnerable Hoosiers, it's going to take a team effort. We're all in this together!
Working Together to Flatten the Curve
It's important to remember in a time like this, that we will not be living like this forever. This is temporary, but the quicker we work together to flatten the curve, the quicker life will go back to normal. This is not a one-person job. It's crucial we all come together in this difficult time and practice the steps below.
* Social distancing is a necessity right now. The sooner we all come together by staying apart, the slower this virus moves -- which means fewer infected people, a slower spread and less pressure on our hospitals.
* Hand washing is important to help prevent the spread of germs. Practice washing your hands often with soap and water, and for 20 seconds at a time. Here's a good reminder if you need some tips (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/photos/a.588449041289491/1743116539156063/?type=3&theater&mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) on how to wash your hands, Hoosier-style.
* Wash/sanitize surfaces as often as possible. Disinfecting also helps to stop the spread of germs.
* Look out for each other now more than ever! While you're at home, call and check on your friends, families, and neighbors. We're all forced to be away from some of our family right now, so let's all look out for one another.
* Consider donating blood with the Red Cross. Many blood drives have been canceled and the Red Cross is in desperate need amid the COVID-19 pandemic. First Lady Janet Holcomb donated blood (https://www.facebook.com/FLJanetHolcomb/videos/896762784129915/?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and you can, too (watch video! (https://www.facebook.com/FLJanetHolcomb/videos/896762784129915/?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) )! Click here (https://www.redcrossblood.org/?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) for more information regarding location and schedule.

http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Join Us at the 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention

The 2020 Indiana Republican State Convention is still on! Even with the new Primary Day of June 2, we still plan to hold our State Convention on June 19-20. For updates, follow us on social media or check your inbox every Friday.

While you’re socially distancing, now is a great time to plan ahead for your convention plans in June:
* Hotel Room Block: The room block for two downtown hotels is now open! If you plan to stay overnight in Indianapolis, reserve your room now! More info is available here (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
* Convention Schedule: Check out the official schedule of events for the two-day convention here (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
* Delegate Fees: If you're in a non-contested race for a delegate spot to this year's convention or if your county chair appoints you to an open delegate spot, you can now pay your delegate fees here (http://indiana.gop/stateconvention?mc_cid=dd1997f44d&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

name: Team Mark Warner
subject: Protecting gig workers during COVID-19
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---
| | | If you would like more information about how to slow and stop the spread of COVID-19, **[take a look at these resources from the Center for Disease Control (CDC).](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d571/53d9897e/5328d593/1c899663/3830823398/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/index.html")**
---

Derek --

As we face the spread of coronavirus (COVID-19), Team Warner has an update on what Mark is doing to protect Virginians and all Americans. Earlier this week, he voted to pass the bipartisan coronavirus emergency relief bill to provide targeted relief for Americans impacted by the outbreak.

**Mark believes that no one should lose their job or miss a paycheck because of this outbreak.** That's why he's been working to pass bipartisan emergency assistance for workers and small businesses. But he also believes that the measures taken to address coronavirus must include our gig economy workers -- independent contractors who work for companies like food delivery and rideshare services.

These workers need a social safety net, plain and simple. **Mark is leading the charge to make sure that happens, and he has already delivered results.** He sent a letter to the largest gig worker platforms calling on these companies to provide financial assistance and flexibility to workers affected by the coronavirus outbreak. So far Uber, Lyft, Postmates, Instacart, and DoorDash have all announced plans to assist gig workers who are quarantined or treated during the Coronavirus outbreak. This helps secure not only their employees' financial security but also our broader public health.

At this point, we have all heard that we should be practicing social distancing or even self-quarantining to reduce the spread of COVID-19. However, following these public health directives may present a financial challenge for some workers. Gig workers are extremely vulnerable during this outbreak -- fear of lost income or high out-of-pocket health care costs may prevent them from staying home from work.

Thousands of Virginians work or supplement their income using these platforms, and many more rely on their services. For years, Mark has been a leader in working to expand our social safety net to cover these workers. And as we continue to deal with this crisis, he's going to keep pushing to make sure federal assistance is available to these and other workers and small business owners who need it.

Thank you for your support,

Team Warner
|
---

[](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d571/53d9897e/5328d593/1c899662/3830823398/VEsE/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://markwarnerva.com") Friends of Mark Warner
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213
Williamsburg, VA 23185

[Unsubscribe](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d571/53d9897e/5328d593/1c899661/3830823398/VEsF/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://act.markwarnerva.com/unsubscribe")

| | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
---

name: Joe Kennedy III
subject: How you can help Massachusetts' tipped and service workers.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900c3/55b66746/23c10c2c/80e262e/265739549/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | Thank you. During a time of crisis, our community has already raised thousands of dollars for frontline organizations fighting COVID-19 here in Massachusetts. I am so proud to be a part of it, to watch this team come together with such incredible generosity and support for those most in need. As you already know - we temporarily shut down our campaign's political operations in the face of this crisis. But that doesn't mean we're putting our online platforms to sleep. Instead, we're leveraging them to help our Commonwealth and country get through this challenging time. We want to continue highlighting some of the folks who need our help most: the tipped and service workers our entire economy depends on. **Donate to the One Fair Wage Fund** Join me in giving to folks facing economic hardship
as restaurants and businesses are forced to close their doors. [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900c3/55b66746/23c10c2c/80e262f/265739549/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIn0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-onefairwage?refcode=2020-03-20-onewage-nao&recurring=1&amount=3") Bartenders, servers, and gig economy workers are being profoundly impacted by this pandemic. I'm doing everything in my power to get them relief on Capitol Hill, fighting for expanded unemployment benefits, small business assistance, and immediate cash payments so no one has to worry about putting food on the table or keeping their roof over their heads during this crisis. Meanwhile, frontline organizations like One Fair Wage Fund play a crucial role in keeping families and workers afloat. Massachusetts' tipped and service workers have served us all - let's pay them back. Hang in there. Be safe. More soon, **Donate to the One Fair Wage Fund** [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/363900c3/55b66746/23c10c2c/80e262f/265739549/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIn0%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-onefairwage?refcode=2020-03-20-onewage-nao&recurring=1&amount=3") |

name: Defeat Senator Cornyn
subject: What's at stake in this race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
If he's reelected, Senator Cornyn WILL try to gut our health care again.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890218/7f51f7bc/4c1232c2/4ef1e925/2321570216/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Now more than ever, health care is at the front of every Texan's mind. Look Peter, if he's reelected, Senator Cornyn WILL try to gut our health care again. He's already voted to repeal the Affordable Care Act **_20 times!_ [Can we count on you to help protect our health care by pitching in to help MJ defeat Senator Cornyn?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890218/7f51f7bc/4c1232c2/4ef1e924/2321570216/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODAifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.2nd__1080__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

**Senator Cornyn has made it clear he wants to gut protections for people with pre-existing conditions.** He supported a reckless lawsuit to strike down the _entire_ Affordable Care Act and increase costs for Texans who need coverage most. We've got to stop him, and MJ is the person to do it.

As a combat veteran who took gunfire during her service and as a working mother who went through two difficult pregnancies, MJ understands just how important healthcare access is. **She flew medevac rescue missions in Afghanistan, standing by her team to make sure wounded soldiers got the medical care they needed --[now she needs your help to make quality, affordable health care available for all Texans.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890218/7f51f7bc/4c1232c2/4ef1e924/2321570216/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjEwODAifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-lessthan1yr?refcode=email_20200318.2nd__1080__&amount=10&recurring=true")** | If we work together, we can beat Senator Cornyn and his allies in Big Pharma and the insurance industry to make sure all Texans have the health care they need. Here are a few key reasons it's so important to win in November:
* **Senator Cornyn voted against a bill to lower prescription drug costs.**
* **He voted to allow insurance companies to charge older Texans up to five times more for coverage.**
* **He supported a lawsuit that would strike down the entire ACA, including protections for people with pre-existing conditions!**

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Checking in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
I want to take a break for a moment and check in with you, folks.

I know how difficult this time is for so many families. All day, every day, I've been thinking about how this is affecting people.

It's okay to be scared right now -- people are losing their jobs, small businesses are closing, seniors are concerned about their health, and families across the country are worried about what comes next.

I'm fighting to give Colorado the strong leadership we deserve right now. Sometimes, that starts with listening.

So, I'm making sure I hear directly from everyday families about how this crisis is affecting them. And I want to hear from you, too.

I want to know how the Coronavirus (COVID-19) has affected you and your loved ones. If you feel comfortable, please share your story here.

SHARE YOUR STORY: https://secure.ngpvan.com/onlineactions/81UqGiMxNUSLeyVL9GD7JA2

I will never stop fighting to keep Coloradans safe.

If you're willing, we'd love to share these stories, too. Chances are someone else is experiencing something similar to what you're going through. Even if we're all keeping our distance from each other, it doesn't mean we have to go through this alone.

Please take care of yourself and your loved ones during this emergency.

Sending all my best,

John
-------

Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado

P.O. Box 18886, Denver, CO 80218

Contributions or gifts to Hickenlooper for Colorado are not tax deductible.

If you got this message from a friend, you can join the Hickenlooper for Colorado team by signing up right here: https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/sign-up

We love staying connected to you—and these emails are the best way to stay up to date with the latest on this campaign. But if at any point you’d like to stop receiving these messages, you can unsubscribe here: https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe. If you'd like to receive only the most important campaign updates, click here: https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/id-like-to-get-less-email.

If we’re not reaching you at the correct email address, please update your information here: https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/sign-up
